basic_details.job_title: ACTREC Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for Medical Physicist, Scientific Officer and More Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Advanced Centre for Treatment, Research and Education in Cancer (ACTREC)
basic_details.short_information: The Advanced Centre for Treatment, Research and Education in Cancer (ACTREC) has officially announced the Various Scientific Posts Recruitment 2026 with 8 openings. The detailed notification was published under Advt No. ACTREC/ADVT/A-08/2026 at the official website. Candidates holding qualifications such as M.Sc. (Physics) with Diploma in Radiological Physics, Ph.D. in Life Sciences/Health Sciences, B.Sc. with relevant certificate/diploma courses, Medical Laboratory Technology, Radiological Imaging Technology, and other related qualifications can submit their online applications before 07-08-2

age_limit.age_details: Please refer to the official notification for Age Limit details.
age_limit.details: MEDICAL PHYSICIST: 45 Years SCIENTIFIC OFFICER 'D' (CLINICIAN SCIENTIST LAB): 35 Years SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (ANESTHESIA): 30 Years SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (HEMATOPATHOLOGY): 30 Years SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (PATHOLOGY): 30 Years SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (RADIODIAGNOSIS): 30 Years | Age Relaxation | 05 Years for SC/ST 03 Years for OBC 10 Years for PWD Ex-servicemen as per Govt. of India rules 5 years for TMC employees

qualification.education_qualification: M.Sc. (Physics) and Diploma in Radiological Physics, Ph.D. in Life Sciences or Health Sciences, B.Sc. with 50% marks and one year certificate/Diploma in ICU/OT/Respiratory/Dialysis Techniques, B.Sc. in Biological Sciences with Degree/Diploma in Medical Laboratory Technology, B.Sc. (Botany/Zoology/Chemistry/Applied Biology/Biology/Biotechnology) with DMLT, B.Sc. (Radiological Imaging Technology) or equivalent with Diploma in Medical Imaging Technology
qualification.details: MEDICAL PHYSICIST 'E': M.Sc. (Physics) and Diploma in Radiological Physics conducted by BARC or Equivalent AERB approved qualifications & Certification of Radiological Safety Officer from AERB SCIENTIFIC OFFICER 'D' (CLINICIAN SCIENTIST LAB): Ph.D. in Life Sciences or Health Sciences SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (ANESTHESIA): B.Sc. with 50 % marks and one year certificate / Diploma course in ICU / OT Techniques / Respiratory therapy Techniques / Dialysis Techniques OR Bachelor degree in paramedical Technology - OT Techniques / bachelor degree in Respiratory Therapy / or Equivalent with minimum 50 % marks in bachelor degree SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (HEMATOPATHOLOGY): B.Sc. in Biological Sciences including Biotechnology with 50% marks and Degree or Diploma in Medical Laboratory Technology or equivalent from a Government recognized Institute with minimum one year working experience in busy Haemato-pathology Laboratory OR B.SC (MLT) with 50% marks and two years experience in busy Haemato-pathology Laboratory SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (PATHOLOGY): B.Sc. (Botany / Zoology / Chemistry / Applied Biology / Biology / Biotechnology) with 50% marks and Degree or Diploma in Medical Laboratory Technology (DMLT) from a Government recognized Institute OR B.Sc. (MLT) from a Government recognized Institute / University SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (RADIODIAGNOSIS): B.Sc. (Radiological Imaging Technology) from a recognized University with 50% marks OR B.Sc. in any subject / B. Pharmacy with 50% marks and minimum two years' Diploma in Medical Imaging Technology from State Board of Technical Education OR Any equivalent recognized Board/ University

salary_details.details: MEDICAL PHYSICIST 'E': Rs. 78800/- Level -12, Cell 1 + Allowances applicable SCIENTIFIC OFFICER 'D' (CLINICIAN SCIENTIST LAB): Rs.67700/- Level -11, Cell 1 + Allowances applicable SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (ANESTHESIA): Rs.35400/- Level -6, Cell 1 + Allowances applicable SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (HEMATOPATHOLOGY): Rs.35400/- Level -6, Cell 1 + Allowances applicable SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (PATHOLOGY): Rs.35400/- Level -6, Cell 1 + Allowances applicable SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (RADIODIAGNOSIS): Rs.35400/- Level -6, Cell 1 + Allowances applicable

how_to_apply: Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online for the ACTREC Various Posts Recruitment 2026.
Applications must be submitted online on or before 07-08-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of ACTREC to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

rows.advanced_centre_for_treatment_research_a: M.Sc. (Physics) and Diploma in Radiological Physics, Ph.D. in Life Sciences or Health Sciences, B.Sc. with 50% marks and one year certificate/Diploma in ICU/OT/Respiratory/Dialysis Techniques, B.Sc. in Biological Sciences with Degree/Diploma in Medical Laboratory Technology, B.Sc. (Botany/Zoology/Chemistry/Applied Biology/Biology/Biotechnology) with DMLT, B.Sc. (Radiological Imaging Technology) or equivalent with Diploma in Medical Imaging Technology
Please refer to the official notification for Age Limit details.

content_sections.heading: ACTREC Vacancy 2026 Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Candidates should download and read the ACTREC Jobs 2026 notification PDF carefully to check vacancy details, eligibility, age limit, and application instructions. The notification also includes important dates and selection process details. The direct download link is available in the Important Links section below. Download ACTREC Job 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: MEDICAL PHYSICIST 'E': M.Sc. (Physics) and Diploma in Radiological Physics conducted by BARC or Equivalent AERB approved qualifications & Certification of Radiological Safety Officer from AERB SCIENTIFIC OFFICER 'D' (CLINICIAN SCIENTIST LAB): Ph.D. in Life Sciences or Health Sciences SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (ANESTHESIA): B.Sc. with 50 % marks and one year certificate / Diploma course in ICU / OT Techniques / Respiratory therapy Techniques / Dialysis Techniques OR Bachelor degree in paramedical Technology - OT Techniques / bachelor degree in Respiratory Therapy / or Equivalent with minimum 50 % marks in bachelor degree SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (HEMATOPATHOLOGY): B.Sc. in Biological Sciences including Biotechnology with 50% marks and Degree or Diploma in Medical Laboratory Technology or equivalent from a Government recognized Institute with minimum one year working experience in busy Haemato-pathology Laboratory OR B.SC (MLT) with 50% marks and two years experience in busy Haemato-pathology Laboratory SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (PATHOLOGY): B.Sc. (Botany / Zoology / Chemistry / Applied Biology / Biology / Biotechnology) with 50% marks and Degree or Diploma in Medical Laboratory Technology (DMLT) from a Government recognized Institute OR B.Sc. (MLT) from a Government recognized Institute / University SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (RADIODIAGNOSIS): B.Sc. (Radiological Imaging Technology) from a recognized University with 50% marks OR B.Sc. in any subject / B. Pharmacy with 50% marks and minimum two years' Diploma in Medical Imaging Technology from State Board of Technical Education OR Any equivalent recognized Board/ University

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: MEDICAL PHYSICIST: 45 Years SCIENTIFIC OFFICER 'D' (CLINICIAN SCIENTIST LAB): 35 Years SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (ANESTHESIA): 30 Years SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (HEMATOPATHOLOGY): 30 Years SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (PATHOLOGY): 30 Years SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (RADIODIAGNOSIS): 30 Years Age Relaxation 05 Years for SC/ST 03 Years for OBC 10 Years for PWD Ex-servicemen as per Govt. of India rules 5 years for TMC employees

content_sections.heading: Salary / Stipend
content_sections.text: MEDICAL PHYSICIST 'E': Rs. 78800/- Level -12, Cell 1 + Allowances applicable SCIENTIFIC OFFICER 'D' (CLINICIAN SCIENTIST LAB): Rs.67700/- Level -11, Cell 1 + Allowances applicable SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (ANESTHESIA): Rs.35400/- Level -6, Cell 1 + Allowances applicable SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (HEMATOPATHOLOGY): Rs.35400/- Level -6, Cell 1 + Allowances applicable SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (PATHOLOGY): Rs.35400/- Level -6, Cell 1 + Allowances applicable SCIENTIFIC ASSISTANT 'B' (RADIODIAGNOSIS): Rs.35400/- Level -6, Cell 1 + Allowances applicable

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online for the ACTREC Various Posts Recruitment 2026. Applications must be submitted online on or before 07-08-2026. To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section. Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of ACTREC to complete the application. Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details. Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format. Pay the application fee online, if applicable. Review all details carefully before submitting the form. Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for ACTREC Various Scientific Posts?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for ACTREC Various Scientific Posts is 07 August 2026. Candidates are advised to apply well before the deadline to avoid any last-minute issues.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are released for ACTREC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 8 vacancies have been released for Various Scientific Posts under the ACTREC Recruitment 2026. These vacancies are distributed across different scientific roles within the organization.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application mode for ACTREC Various Scientific Posts?
faq.answer: The application mode for ACTREC Various Scientific Posts is Online. Interested and eligible candidates must submit their applications through the official ACTREC website.

faq.question: Q4. What is the official website for ACTREC Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The official website for Advanced Centre for Treatment, Research and Education in Cancer (ACTREC) recruitment is https://actrec.gov.in. All official notifications and application links will be available there.

faq.question: Q5. What are the application fees for General/OBC candidates?
faq.answer: General and OBC category candidates are required to pay an application fee of Rs. 300/-. However, SC/ST and Women candidates are exempted from paying any application fee.

faq.question: Q6. What qualifications are required for Medical Physicist 'E' post?
faq.answer: For the Medical Physicist 'E' post, candidates need an M.Sc. (Physics) and a Diploma in Radiological Physics conducted by BARC or equivalent AERB approved qualifications, along with Certification of Radiological Safety Officer from AERB.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary for Scientific Assistant 'B' posts?
faq.answer: The salary for Scientific Assistant 'B' posts (Anesthesia, Hematopathology, Pathology, Radiodiagnosis) is Rs. 35400/- at Level -6, Cell 1, plus applicable allowances. This is a competitive salary for these roles.

faq.question: Q8. Where is the job location for ACTREC recruitment?
faq.answer: The job location for the ACTREC recruitment is Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra. ACTREC is a premier research institute located in this region, offering excellent career opportunities.

faq.question: Q9. What is the selection process for ACTREC Various Scientific Posts?
faq.answer: The selection process for ACTREC Various Scientific Posts includes an Interview, a Written Examination, and Document Verification. Candidates must perform well in all stages to be considered for selection.

faq.question: Q10. Is there any age relaxation for reserved categories?
faq.answer: Yes, age relaxation is provided for reserved categories. SC/ST candidates receive 5 years, OBC candidates 3 years, and PWD candidates 10 years of relaxation. Ex-servicemen and TMC employees also receive relaxation as per government rules.

faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application for ACTREC Various Scientific Posts is 07 August 2026. Candidates are advised to apply well before the deadline to avoid any last-minute issues.
